section.heading: Promotion of lending for environment
section.chapeau: The Secretary of the Treasury shall instruct the United States Executive Director of the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development to initiate discussions with the other executive directors of such bank and the management of such bank and propose that, in order to reduce the future need for bank lending for reforestation and restoration of environmentally degraded areas, the bank establish a project and policy based environmental lending program (including a loan a portion of which could be used to reduce and restructure private debt), to be made available to interested countries with a demonstrated commitment to natural resource conservation, which would be based on—

paragraph.content: the estimated long-term economic return which could be expected from the sustainable use and protection of tropical forests, including the value of tropical forests for indigenous people and for science;

paragraph.chapeau: the value derived from such services as—

clause.content: water supply regulation for industrial development;

subparagraph.content: the extraction of naturally occurring products from locally controlled protected areas; and
indigenous knowledge of the management and use of natural resources; and

paragraph.content: the long-term benefits expected to be derived from maintaining biological diversity and climate stabilization.

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 95–118, title XVI, § 1615, as added Pub. L. 101–240, title V, § 512, Dec. 19, 1989, 103 Stat. 2510.)

note.heading: Definitions
note.p: The definitions in section 262p–5 of this title apply to this section.
